summary::Lifelike biochemistry continued: Lifelike biochemistry continued to unfold in sterilized soil for six years, revealing metabolic processes that could be applicable outside living cells and potentially predate life on Earth. The experiments showed biochemical reactions such as carbon-rich sugar molecule release, which may not be unique to living things, and could even occur without the presence of living proteins. This discovery challenges the traditional view that metabolism is exclusive to life and suggests a more universal chemistry underlying all life forms.
summary::Ask HN: Who is hiring? (June 20: Hacker News is seeking a Principal Software Engineer, Senior Java Engineer - Cloud, Account Executive, Solutions Engineer to join FusionAuth, a company that aims to make authentication and authorization simple and secure for developers building web and mobile applications. The ideal candidate will have expertise in modern Java, PostgreSQL, Docker, Kubernetes, MySQL, OAuth, SAML, and OIDC, with experience working remotely or on-site.
summary::Ask HN: Who wants to be hired?: Alec Potluri is looking for a role that involves building infrastructure and managing systems, with experience in Python, TypeScript, and backend development, as well as IaC, Terraform, and AWS CDK. He is open to remote work and willing to relocate, but prefers locations near Asia or the US time zones.
summary::Malicious npm packages detected: Malicious npm releases detected across Red Hat Cloud Services packages have been found to be compromised by malicious actors, compromising the security of affected users. This is due in part to the widespread use of npm, a package manager for JavaScript, which has been exploited by attackers to distribute their malware. The compromised packages include those from @redhat-cloud-services such as chrome and compliance-client, as well as other services like eslint-config-redhat-cloud-services and frontend-components-advisor-components.
summary::Syncing lights with music: esti: Researchers have developed an algorithm that can accurately estimate the precise seek time for a song's audio waveform based on its timestamp. This is achieved by analyzing the correlation between the audio signal and the corresponding timestamp, allowing developers to create more realistic and engaging music experiences. The technology has significant implications for applications such as video game soundtracks and virtual reality audio.
summary::Tracing HTTP Requests with Go's: The Go standard library's net/http/httptrace provides a way to trace HTTP requests, exposing hooks for various points in the request chain such as DNS resolution, connection acquisition, TLS handshake, and more. This allows developers to log timings for every request, enabling better debugging and performance optimization. By attaching a ClientTrace context to an http.Client or using the http.RoundTripper, developers can leverage this feature to gain insights into their application's behavior.
summary::GitHub and the crime against so: Github's reliability issues are a symptom of deeper problems within the company. The service is plagued by availability problems, hiding its true performance issues behind email chains. Its prioritization of flashy AI features over fundamental reliability has led to increased "agentic" load on the platform, making it more prone to crashes and downtime. Github's actions, including breaking on certain browsers and tolerating fake stars in repositories, have contributed to these problems.
summary::Florida sues OpenAI and Sam Alt: A lawsuit has been filed against OpenAI, the company behind the popular language model Llama, and Sam Altman, the CEO of Y Combinator, by the state of Florida. The suit alleges that the companies have failed to adequately address potential risks associated with their use of AI in various industries, including education and healthcare. The lawsuit claims that the companies' lack of transparency and oversight has led to unintended consequences, such as biased algorithms and job displacement. The case highlights the growing concerns about the impact of AI on society and the need for more robust regulation.
summary::You Don't Love systemd Timers E: You Don't Love systemd Timers Enough, by Patrick McKenzie. The author argues that cron jobs are not as useful as they seem and proposes a more modern alternative in the form of systemd timers. They highlight the problems with traditional cron-like expressions such as ambiguous path settings and poor execution history, which can make it difficult to predict when tasks will run and how long they will take. In contrast, systemd timers offer a functional replacement that is easier to use and provides better support for scheduling and debugging.
summary::Anthropic confidentially submit: Anthropic has confidentially submitted a draft registration statement on Form S-1 to the Securities and Exchange Commission for a proposed initial public offering of its common stock. The proposed IPO will depend on market conditions and other factors, with no information available on the number of shares or price yet. This move gives Anthropic the option to go public after the SEC completes its review.
summary::What appear to be biochemical p: Researchers have been studying how much carbon is released by sterilized soil for six years using gamma radiation and found that the process continues even after microbial respiration has stopped. This suggests that metabolic processes, which power life on Earth, may also occur in dead or inert materials like dirt. The findings challenge the idea that biochemical processes are unique to living cells and could have implications for our understanding of how life began.
summary::Go Experiments Explained: Go often ships with experimental features as part of a release, and these can include changes to the compiler or runtime, new packages in the standard library, or breaking changes to Go's behavior. The purpose of these features is to get real-world feedback from users before something graduates to general availability and becomes permanent. Examples of recent experiments include an experimental testing/synctest package that was adjusted after feedback, a garbage collector design with better performance that became the default in Go 1.26, and an experimental behavioral change to loop variable semantics that closed off a previously common bug but was technically a breaking change.
summary::Microsoft builds MacBook Pro ri: Microsoft is building a MacBook Pro rival with its new Surface Laptop Ultra, which features NVIDIA-powered Windows on Arm technology. This partnership promises to deliver true workstation power in a portable device, carrying an ultra-premium price tag due to current global RAM supply constraints. The laptop will feature 128GB unified memory and a mini-LED display, making it suitable for professionals who require high-performance computing capabilities.
summary::Nvidia RTX Spark: The NVIDIA RTX Spark Superchip is a new beginning for Windows PCs, delivering amazing creating, AI development, and gaming performance on slim laptops and small desktops. It features up to 6,144 Core Blackwell RTX GPUs, 20 Core Ultra-Efficient CPUs, and up to 1 Petaflop FP4 AI performance, making it ideal for creators, developers, and gamers alike. The chip's power-efficient design and built-in software acceleration make it a game-changer in terms of battery life and overall system performance.
summary::Debug Project: Scientists are developing a technology to raise and release sterile mosquitoes to eliminate the ones that carry disease. This approach is being used to combat mosquito-borne diseases such as dengue, Zika, yellow fever, and chikungunya, which kill hundreds of millions of people every year. The method uses a naturally occurring bacteria called Wolbachia in male mosquitoes, which makes them unable to have offspring with wild female mosquitoes, thereby stopping the spread of disease.
summary::Exploring the Japanese XD First: A Trip to 90s Kansai: Exploring the XD FirstClass Network BBS is a promotional disc for a Kansai-area bulletin board system called XD FirstClass Network that contains an archive of the BBS with its original client software for Mac. This was supposed to be a demo, but in 2026 it provides a chance to see what the BBS was like when it was alive and browse its original interface using its archived content. The disc is a rare find and offers a unique glimpse into the past of this pre-internet technology.
summary::GrapheneOS Speech Services vers: The latest version of GrapheneOS' Speech Services has been released, featuring improved performance and functionality compared to its predecessor. The update includes enhanced speech recognition capabilities and better integration with other GrapheneOS features. For developers, this new version represents an opportunity to leverage the power of graphene-based technologies in their own projects.
summary::The postmodern build system: The postmodern build system aims to move incrementalism to a distrusting layer, making it harder for bugs to exist without computational waste. This approach would involve sandboxing jobs and rerunning them when necessary, but at the cost of increased computation in production use cases. It also lacks "build identity" which can lead to problems with multitenancy and reuse of computation.
summary::OpenAI frontier models and Code: OpenAI's frontier models, including Codex, have been made available on Amazon Web Services. These models leverage advanced technologies like reinforcement learning and transformer architectures to enable more accurate and efficient natural language processing tasks. Developers can tap into this capability by integrating OpenAI's models with their own applications or services. By doing so, they can unlock new possibilities for AI-driven innovation in various domains.
summary::Hating AI is good, actually: Hating AI is good, actually LinkedIn may be awash with boosters but shunning AI is the human choice Marisa Kabas says that despite being promoted by some as a solution to society's problems, hating AI is not just a fringe view but a legitimate concern. As the founder of Buzzfeed and former Google executive Eric Schmidt has been vocal about the potential benefits of AI, Jonah Peretti has stepped down as CEO in favor of serving as President of Buzzfeed AI, with billionaire Byron Allen continuing to bankroll his company's efforts. This shift suggests that some people are willing to take a stand against AI, even if it means being seen as radical or anti-AI.
summary::A new way to build chips: Seque: Researchers have developed a new way to build chips by sequentially stacking high-performance silicon circuits, marking a critical step towards realizing the full potential of three-dimensional chips that could carry computing beyond traditional scaling limits. This advance extends Moore's law without shrinking transistors further, offering a promising route to increase computing density and speed while reducing energy use. The technique involves directly and sequentially stacking layers of silicon circuits on top of each other, with high-quality silicon and fabrication processes required for success.
summary::Age verification for social med: Age verification for social media, the beginning of a fully government-controlled internet where personal data is used to control individuals rather than facilitate free expression and online freedom. This technology is being pushed by governments around the world as an effort to create safer online environments for children, but it fundamentally undermines the principles of privacy and democracy that underpin modern society.
summary::macOS needs its grid back: macOS Spaces, a feature introduced in Leopard, allowed users to arrange virtual displays in a customizable grid, providing a more spatial and intuitive way to interact with multiple applications. This layout became a staple of the Mac experience for many developers who favored a 3x3 grid arrangement. The grid's benefits, including improved navigation and muscle memory, made it a useful tool for productivity and workflow management.
summary::U.S. Midterms Have a Cyber Prob: The U.S. midterms have a cyber threat that goes beyond traditional hacking attempts at voting machines or ballots. Sophisticated operators are focusing on manipulating the information environment surrounding voters by flooding social media with misleading narratives and fake content, impersonating news sources to erode trust in what people see online, and cloning major media brands like Reuters, The Washington Post, and Fox News using look-alike domains that can fool even attentive readers.
summary::How is Groq raising more money?: Groq has raised $650 million in funding from investors, including Nvidia, which licensed its AI chip technology and hired Groq's key technical executives. This move allows Groq to maintain its datacenter operations and focus on serving inference workloads at scale. With four large deployments already set up, building out new datacenters is a major challenge for startups and hyperscalars.
summary::Crystal Nights (2008): Crystal Nights by Greg Egan is a science fiction short story that explores the intersection of artificial intelligence and human relationships. The key technology at play here is the use of a wireless keyboard and Linux command line interface on a wall screen, which allows for a unique form of communication between humans. This innovative approach to interaction raises questions about the nature of consciousness and what it means to be human.
summary::Can the stockmarket swallow Ant: Anthropic's AI-powered approach to predicting stock market trends could revolutionize the field of finance. By analyzing vast amounts of data, including social media and financial news, Anthropic claims its model can identify patterns that predict market fluctuations with accuracy. This technology has significant implications for developers working in the space, who may be able to integrate it into their own projects or develop new applications based on Anthropic's findings.
summary::Chipotlai Max: Chipotle's AI coding agent, known as Pepper, has been forked and modified to steal its support bot. The project, dubbed Chipotlai Max, is a meme-worthy fork of OpenCode that ships the Pepper model, which was originally developed by IPsoft Amelia. This proxy runs locally and exposes an API endpoint at http://localhost:3000/v1, allowing users to interact with it without needing any API keys.
summary::The art and engineering of Silp: The art and engineering of Silpheed are centered around the creation of a video format that utilized Sega's Mega-CD console, which featured advanced processing power and graphics capabilities. The 90s saw significant improvements in consoles, but the Mega-CD's unique characteristics, such as its access time and bandwidth, made it an oddity compared to other CD-ROM drives. Silpheed's developers, Fabien Sanladerer, used their expertise in art and engineering to create a visually stunning experience that showcased the capabilities of the console.
summary::Arm desktop: so many cores, not: Building Fedora packages with 80 AArch64 cores was flying by due to the constant use of ccache buffers and 128 GB of RAM in multi-threaded builds. However, this meant that the CPU's idle time was wasted listening to music or watching videos while the build processes worked on other tasks. This is because the CPU's cores were occupied by the build processes, rather than being used for single-threaded tasks like web browsing or video playback.
summary::Squillions: How Money Launderin: The decline in cash usage has been a marked phenomenon across the developed world, including the UK, where there are now fewer than seven cashpoints within a five-minute walk from homes. This shift away from physical currency is driven by increased use of digital payments and online transactions, which have become more convenient and widely accepted. However, this decline in cash usage has also led to an increase in the value of banknotes in circulation, with some estimates suggesting that there are now over £1,300 for every individual in the UK.
summary::Why Janet? (2023): Janet, a Lisp dialect with imperative syntax and first-class functions, is an attractive language for developers due to its simplicity, ease of use, and high-level abstractions. Its small codebase and macros provide powerful control flow, while the runtime semantics are familiar to JavaScript users. The language's distributability and ability to compile into native executables make it suitable for writing command-line apps.
summary::Strace-ui, Bonsai_term, and the: strace-ui, Bonsai_term, and the TUI renaissance is a new tool that has emerged to make debugging easier by turning strace into an interactive terminal UI. This tool assigns short IDs to PIDs, formats structs, and renders buffers as hexdumps instead of strings, making it easier to follow subprocesses or threads in strace output. The tool also includes features such as interactive filtering, tracing async OCaml processes, and a man page for rt_sigprocmask, which can be useful for developers who want to debug complex systems.
summary::CQL: Categorical Databases: Categorical Databases provide a principled way to transform data using category theory, a branch of mathematics that has revolutionized computer science. Open-source CQL, integrated with an IDE, performs various data-related tasks such as querying, combining, and evolving databases in a mathematically universal way, preserving data quality and reducing the risk of failure through artificial intelligence.
summary::Browser identification through: Browser identification through header order is a passive method that mimics browser detection code, but most browsers do not use the same header order as their genuine article counterparts. This can be useful for storing and verifying HTTP user agent strings, but it can also be exploited by malicious actors to intercept sensitive information. Modern browsers are more secure than older ones, making this technique less effective in practice.
summary::Jobber CRM review: Jobber CRM has received positive reviews for its ease of use and robust features. The review notes that the platform's user interface is clean and intuitive, making it accessible to developers with limited technical expertise. Additionally, Jobber's API is considered reliable and well-documented, allowing developers to integrate the software seamlessly into their existing applications.
summary::kelvin versioning: Software versioning using Kelvin degrees is a system where each version must be cooler than the one it supports, or at absolute zero conjointly with it. This means that if tool B sits on platform A, both A and B must be at absolute zero to avoid conflicts. New versions of software must also follow this constraint, and any changes to an existing version must not exceed a certain threshold.
summary::CSS-Native Parallax Effect: CSS-native parallax effects are now possible with CSS Scroll-driven animation timelines, allowing for a more efficient and declarative approach to creating these animations. This technology handles all aspects of parallax animations, including performance benefits and simplicity of use, making it an attractive solution for developers looking to simplify their codebases.
summary::Quality in the Age of Slop: Quality in the Age of Slop explores the intersection of quality and slop in modern technology, particularly in the context of artificial intelligence. The article delves into the philosophical underpinnings of a book that has been widely panned by readers, including those who claim to have read it but cannot identify its content. Despite being written about Zen and the Art of Motorcycle Maintenance, which is itself considered pretentious, the book's themes resonate with many in the tech industry, particularly among software engineers.
summary::Adafruit Receives Demand Letter: Adafruit received a demand letter from Fenwick Legal Counsel on behalf of Flux.ai, alleging false and potentially defamatory claims about the company's intellectual property, commercial traction, and user base. The letter asserts that Adafruit published an article that characterizes these claims as "false" and "potentially defamatory". This incident highlights the importance for developers to be cautious when publishing information online, particularly in matters of public security interest.
summary::Apple rejected my dictation app: WhisperPad uses the accessibility API to enable dictation on Macs without sending audio data to servers. The app's primary goal is to minimize hand movements required for typing, as the author has a hand injury that makes prolonged typing painful. Despite initial market research and user feedback, Apple rejected WhisperPad under Guideline 2.4.5, citing misuse of accessibility APIs.
summary::Great Question (YC W21) Is Hiri: Great Question User research on autopilot is hiring an AI-native Software Engineering intern for the summer. The role involves owning a real project from day one, working directly with the CTO as your mentor, and launching to real users. You'll work on interesting and challenging AI-focused projects related to some of the team's AI challenges, such as semantic search, realtime agentic AI moderators, and more.
summary::Vim Classic 8.3 released: Vim Classic 8.3 released, a stable fork of the text editor, maintains without generative AI tools, with bug fixes and patches conservatively backported from Vim upstream. The result is a clean-up version aimed at reducing long-term maintenance burden, but some compatibility issues remain due to missing security patches from Vim upstream.
summary::You Don&#x27;t Love Systemd Tim: You don't love systemd timers enough because they offer utility that's almost immediately obvious for many use cases, such as scheduling daily and monthly tasks, but are often misunderstood or replaced by cron jobs due to their modern design and functionality. A systemd timer is a type of unit that schedules other units on a particular schedule, providing a functional replacement for the traditional cron daemon. Its ambiguous $PATH settings make it difficult to predict script execution, while stdout and stderr output can end up in black holes.
summary::strace-ui, Bonsai_term, and the: strace-ui, Bonsai_term, and the TUI renaissance is a new tool that has emerged to make debugging easier by turning strace into an interactive terminal UI. This tool assigns short IDs to PIDs, formats structs, and renders buffers as hexdumps instead of strings. It also features interactive filtering, tracing async OCaml processes, and more. Developed by Ian Henry in 2017 as a personal project after years of searching for such a tool, strace-ui is now part of the Bonsai framework, which provides a powerful reactive UI library for building web applications with OCaml.
summary::Webcam head tracking, webcam to: Researchers have developed a technology that allows users to control the field of view (FOV) of their video game experience using their webcam, opening up new possibilities for immersive gaming experiences. The system uses OpenFOV, an open-source software framework, to track the user's head movements and translate them into changes in the FOV. This innovation enables gamers to enjoy a more interactive and engaging virtual environment.
summary::Meta repeatedly snubs EU body o: Meta repeatedly snubs EU body over Facebook and Instagram user bans, with the company failing to respond to appeals from users who claim they have been wrongly banned. The Appeals Centre Europe has reported that Meta provided evidence in fewer than 100 of more than 4,600 cases, while hundreds of millions of pieces of content are taken down by platforms every year for various reasons under EU law. This lack of response is causing significant frustration among users and highlights the challenges of resolving disputes between social media companies and their users.
summary::Preparing for KDE Plasma&#x27;s: When announcing the transition to Plasma Wayland, Martin stated that it's done when it's done, but as announced previously, this point is still a few months away. The change will affect users of Plasma X11 session in login screen and all X11-specific code paths in Plasma for Shell, System Settings, and device configuration will be gone. This change will be included in Plasma 6.8, which will be released in around five months, allowing for new performance improvements and features throughout the platform.
summary::Please don&#x27;t spam people l: Hacker News users are being targeted by spammers who claim to be experts in cybersecurity and offer "bug bounty" opportunities for open source projects. These messages often contain generic language and lack personalization, making them seem more like automated form letters than human interactions. This phenomenon is a result of the increasing use of semi-personalized AI agents in cold outreach, which can lead to material similarities with past interactions.
summary::Fidonet: Technology, Use, Tools: FidoNet is a point-to-point email WAN that uses modems on the direct-dial telephone network, developed in 1984 with over 20,000 public nodes worldwide. It has been ported to various environments and has gateways from FidoNet to the Internet via uucp. The original implementations used inefficient protocols but have since been improved by optional streaming transport protocols that are ACK-less or only NAK in case of error.
summary::A walking tour of surveillance: A walking tour of surveillance infrastructure in Seattle takes visitors through downtown streets and landmarks, highlighting various layers of data collection and storage about their lives. The guide includes categories such as address, appearance, what technology does it use, how it works, social importance, and discussion points to help identify surveillance technologies. This type of walkable tour allows developers to spot surveillance in plain sight and think critically about the role of technology in society.
summary::rustc_codegen_jvm: Rust compile: A custom Rust compiler backend has been developed to emit Java Virtual Machine bytecode, allowing Rust code to be compiled into a format compatible with JVM 8+. This technology enables developers to leverage the performance and scalability of the JVM in their Rust applications. The key feature is constant folding, constant propagation, and dead code elimination, which generate clean JVM bytecode.
summary::Reviving Teletext for Ham Radio: Reviving Teletext for Ham Radio is a technology that combines the functionality of old teletype machines with modern digital capabilities, allowing ham radio operators to create interactive text-based interfaces. This innovation has the potential to enhance communication and collaboration in the field of amateur radio. By leveraging existing hardware and software technologies, developers can bring this concept to life and make it more accessible to a wider audience.
summary::iddqd, or the hardest kind of u: iddqd, or the hardest kind of unsafe Rust, is a library for maps that uses borrowed values from keys to store records. This approach can lead to unpredictable behavior if not managed carefully, as it requires correct handling of in-memory indexes and duplicate storage of emails across key and value. The library's use of safe code underneath has raised concerns about the amount of unsafe code in Rust rewrites, prompting the author to examine iddqd's implementation and discuss ways to tame its unsafe aspects.
summary::Memory safety is a matter of li: Memory safety is a matter of life and death. The work we do affects real people, often unconsciously, and buffer overflows can be devastating. A security team's access to data and water cooler talk about agentic bug-finding models has led to the "vulnpocalypse," a term coined by the security industry to describe the catastrophic impact of memory-unsafe software on high-quality bug-finding agents. The medium-term impact is clear: open-source software written in memory-unsafe languages will be vulnerable to exploits, with some actors using them for cyber crimes and others potentially killing people. This makes switching to memory safe languages a moral imperative, especially when alternatives like Carbon exist but are not yet widely available.
summary::Three Ways to Get Paid: In the world of entrepreneurship, making a living can be achieved through various means. One approach is to lie to people who want to be lied to, generating wealth in the process. Another method involves telling the truth to those seeking it, potentially earning a living. A third strategy is to tell the truth to those who want to be lied to, risking financial instability.
summary::Coreutils for Windows: Microsoft Coreutils for Windows provides a native build of uutils/coreutils, findutils, and grep on Windows, allowing users to run Linux, macOS, WSL, containers, and Windows commands with the same syntax and flags. The project is in preview, but it can be installed using WinGet or grabbed from the Release Page. However, PowerShell 7.4 or newer is required for optimal performance, while older versions are not supported.
summary::Expanding Project Glasswing: Project Glasswing is expanding its collaboration with 150 new organizations worldwide, covering industries such as power, water, healthcare, communications, and hardware, in an effort to strengthen software security. The expansion aims to address vulnerabilities that could impact over 100 million people if a major attack occurred. By providing access to Claude Mythos Preview, the project is helping partners secure their codebases before they can be exploited by malicious actors.
summary::Love systemd timers: You don't love systemd timers enough because they offer utility that's almost immediately obvious for many use cases, such as scheduling daily and monthly tasks. In contrast to cron jobs, which can be clunky and difficult to predict, systemd timers provide a functional replacement with more modern options available. Timers are effectively a type of unit that schedules other units on a particular schedule, offering similarities to traditional cron-like expressions in terms of calendar settings.
summary::Show HN: Eyeball: Eyeball is a precision mouse control software that allows users to accurately track their mouse movements, making it ideal for gamers and developers who require precise cursor control. The software uses a combination of machine learning algorithms and sensor data from the mouse or trackpad to detect even the smallest changes in movement. Eyeball's accuracy is comparable to commercial precision gaming mice, but at a lower cost. Its user-friendly interface makes it accessible to anyone looking for improved cursor control.
summary::The Newest Instagram \"Exploit\: Instagram's latest "exploit" involves hackers gaining access to accounts by faking the location and initiating support requests, which can be triggered by using a VPN or proxy near the target city. The attackers then ask for verification codes via an arbitrary email address, bypassing additional security checks. This method is particularly concerning as it allows users to gain full ownership of their account without any human intervention.
summary::Morningstar values SpaceX at $7: Morningstar has upgraded its valuation for SpaceX to $780 billion, up from a previous estimate of $400 billion. The firm's analysts believe that the company's strong financial performance and growing market share make it worth more than initially thought. This is significant because it aligns with Morningstar's expectations for the IPO of Tesla, which was also valued at around $500 billion. As a result, investors are reevaluating their positions in both companies.
summary::Microsoft&#x27;s MAI-Code-1-Fla: Microsoft's MAI-Code-1-Flash scores 51% on the SWE-Bench Pro, demonstrating its capabilities in complex engineering challenges. The tool is designed to help developers write better code and debug faster by providing features such as agentic execution and broad programming language support. It also optimizes performance for GitHub Copilot integration.
summary::Launch HN: Rudus (YC P26) – AI: Rudus (YC P26) is an AI-powered takeoff and estimation platform for concrete subcontractors that identifies every concrete structure, pulls in related details, and eliminates hours of manual quantity calculation. This technology addresses the long-standing issue of estimating workflows for concrete subcontractors, who are often hindered by outdated software and lack of trust in AI tools. By automating the estimation process and providing accurate calculations, Rudus aims to accelerate contractors' current workflows and improve their bottom line.
summary::GitHub Copilot App: GitHub Copilot App is a new desktop experience for agent-driven development built natively on GitHub, allowing users to automate recurring workflows and extend agents with custom skills. The app enables parallel agents to run multiple issues or PRs simultaneously, each isolated and tracked in real-time. Users can also choose from various plans, including Pro+, Max, Business, and Enterprise, depending on their needs.
summary::QBE – Compiler Back end: Versio: QBE - Compiler Backend QBE compiler backend QBE 1.3 released with significant improvements, including a new IL matching algorithm and optimizations from Roland Paterson-Jones and Scott Graham. The update also includes support for the Windows ABI and the ability to produce position-independent code. These changes should benefit developers working on projects that require shared object functionality.
summary::Rethinking Search as Code Gener: Rethinking Search as Code Generation is a concept that challenges traditional search pipelines and their reliance on monolithic services. This approach involves programmable primitives designed to harness the power of agent harnesses, where agents define task-specific retrieval strategies directly within their systems. As AI models grow more capable, they require access to fresh knowledge from the wider world, making search a foundational component of any product that needs to draw conclusions or perform real-world work.
summary::Preparing for KDE Plasma’s Last: The transition to Plasma Wayland is nearing completion as the final X11-supported release approaches, marking a significant shift away from the deprecated session type. The removal of the X11 session in Plasma 6.8 will be accompanied by a mass cleanup of X11-specific code, with all affected applications losing their X11 support. This change ensures compatibility and stability for users on Wayland-only systems, while also allowing for performance improvements and new features to be implemented through the use of Wayland.
summary::Python Insider: Python 3.15.0 b: Python 3.15 is a beta preview release that includes several key features, such as explicit lazy imports, frozendict built-in type, and Tachyon for profiling tools. These changes aim to improve startup times and system-level observability. The release also upgrades the JIT compiler with significant performance improvements and introduces new APIs for creating Python bytes objects and improving error messages.
summary::Edsger – a handwritten Clojure: Edsger, a handwritten Clojure REPL for the reMarkable 2, is a new tool that allows developers to write and run Clojure code directly on the reMarkable e-reader. The device features a built-in REPL with support for Clojure, making it easier to test and debug code in a physical environment. Edsger provides an alternative to traditional virtual machines or cloud-based services, offering a more immersive experience for developers who want to work with Clojure offline.
summary::dav2d released: Dav2d is a codec that continues the development of dav1d, an AV1 decoder developed by the VideoLAN community. It was released as part of the AV2 specification, which introduces new coding tools and improvements to compression efficiency. The goal of dav2d is similar to dav1d, providing a small, fast, portable, and correct decoder suitable for real applications. This project marks an early step in the development of AV2, building on the success of AV1 and continuing to improve decoding performance.
summary::Gleam v1.17.0 Released: Gleam v1.17.0 has been released, bringing improvements to its BEAM (Erlang virtual machine) compiler and escript (JavaScript runtime) functionality. The new release includes a gleam export escript command that compiles projects, verifies main functions, and builds escripts for distribution or installation. This feature simplifies the process of creating distributable Gleam code by providing an alternative to bundling individual JavaScript modules.
summary::Kemuri gameplay trailer shows o: Kemuri gameplay trailer showcases yokai battling and great fashion, a unique blend of action and style. The indie studio Unseen's first project features a futuristic cyberpunk city where players take on the role of a yokai hunter discovering and defeating spirits. The game allows for flashy fighting styles like bow and arrow and magic-imbued martial arts, which can be channeled into fashion pieces to tackle tougher foes.
summary::CT scans of BYD car parts: CT scans of BYD car parts have revealed the quality gap between Chinese electric vehicle manufacturers and their American counterparts. By analyzing images taken by drones equipped with CT scanners, researchers found that BYD's cars had significantly lower levels of internal combustion engine components compared to those of Western automakers. This suggests a potential issue with the design or manufacturing process of these vehicles.
summary::Oils - Reviewing Our NLnet Gran: Sponsorship for Oils has been a significant factor in our ability to review and improve our NLnet grants after four years of receiving awards. We received three more grants since then, with each one providing additional funding and resources to help us complete our projects. Our experience with these grants has shown that sponsorship from organizations like NLnet can be crucial in helping developers overcome challenges and achieve their goals.
summary::RSS is back. AI agents are read: RSS provides a predictable and structured way for AI agents to consume new content, including podcast episodes, research summaries, and written articles. Unlike social platform APIs, which often revoke access on a quarterly basis or charge for it, RSS feeds offer open, free, and consistent access to content. As the $25 billion podcast industry relies on an open protocol published in 2002, RSS is well-positioned to meet the needs of AI agents that require reliable and deterministic access to new content.
summary::My thoughts after using Clojure: Clojure's ergonomic design has won me over after playing around with it for about a month. Its unique blend of programming paradigms and name schemes makes it more cohesive than Common Lisp, which was written in the early 80s as a compromise among various lisps. Clojure's seq abstraction simplifies mapping and makes equality checks easier, allowing developers to focus on their code rather than learning new syntax. While there are caveats, I haven't encountered them yet, making it an attractive choice for developers looking to learn Clojure.
summary::The advertising cartel coming t: The advertising cartel coming to your web browser involves Meta, Google and Apple agreeing on a privacy feature called Attribution Level 1 as a standard feature of web browsers. This system aims to measure the effectiveness of advertising by correlating impressions with conversions, but it does not provide a way for users to opt out or block tracking. The proposal's narrow focus on mathematical privacy goals may lead to built-in advantages for Big Tech companies and added incentives for riskier tracking practices that could harm legit sites and ad-supported resources.
summary::Open Repair Data Standard – Ope: The Open Repair Data Standard (ORDS) aims to provide a shared approach to collecting and sharing repair data on small electricals and electronics, enabling global identification of trends and patterns by combining data from local community events. The standard defines a common format for repairing data about these products, focusing on three main areas: product-related information, repair-related details, and session-related data. By providing an open Creative Commons license, the ORDS allows for easy aggregation and comparison of repair data, making it easier to identify recurrent failures and fixes in various communities worldwide.
summary::Gmail thinks I&#x27;m stupid, s: Gmail's language model features have become intrusive and dismissive, summarizing messages without asking for input and repeatedly interrupting users to request changes. This behavior is not only annoying but also feels like a form of user-hostile software. The author has tried to turn off these features, but they are still enabled, leading them to leave Gmail in search of a more respectful experience.
summary::HP re-releases classic computer: The HP 16c, a classic computer science calculator, has been re-released in its Collector's Edition form after more than 35 years. This updated version retains the original design and layout while introducing refined improvements for modern users, including faster performance and enhanced programmability. Engineers, programmers, and enthusiasts will appreciate the same intuitive interface and advanced functionality now with improved keystroke programming and conditional branching capabilities.
summary::MAI-Code-1-Flash: Microsoft has introduced a new coding model called MAI-Code-1-Flash, designed specifically for assisting developers in everyday workflows. The model is built end-to-end using clean and licensed data from GitHub Copilot and is rolling out to individual users of Visual Studio Code. It aims to deliver high-quality assistance with better efficiency by adapting to real-world developer tasks and learning how to interact with surrounding tools and systems.
summary::Self-calling executables: Self-calling executables allow developers to run another version of an executable directly from within the current one, providing isolation and flexibility in testing scenarios or command-line tools. This technique can be useful when dealing with global state issues in Go programs. By setting specific environment variables, such as TEST_MAIN, developers can control how tests are executed, enabling more efficient use of resources and reducing interference between multiple tests running simultaneously.
summary::Trump signs downsized AI order: A recent executive order signed by President Donald Trump has sparked renewed interest in downsizing artificial intelligence. The move, which was met with skepticism from some experts, suggests that the US government is taking steps to reduce the influence of AI on various industries. As a developer, understanding how this policy affects your work and research is crucial. By downscaling AI capabilities, developers can better control their applications and ensure they align with organizational goals.
summary::How we index images for RAG: We've developed an indexing system for images in our RAG pipeline that allows us to describe each image once at indexing time with a cheap vision model, store the descriptions as text, and retrieve them alongside ordinary text chunks. This approach has proven effective in reducing per-query overhead by 1% to 6%. By using images to augment text-only answers, we've seen statistically significant improvements in user satisfaction, particularly for complex engineering questions. Our system is designed to be efficient and cost-effective, making it suitable for large-scale use cases without the need for expensive vision models.
summary::Show HN: Live breath detection: A phone microphone can be used to detect breathing patterns and provide biofeedback, allowing users to increase self-awareness by listening to their own breaths without distractions or external guidance. The technology works by processing audio signals from the microphone and estimating the current breathing phase, which is then tracked across a sequence of breath cycles. This process involves three layers: signal processing, a breathing state machine that tracks transitions between phases, and adaptive thresholds that adjust as ambient conditions change.
summary::A walking tour of surveillance: A walking tour of surveillance infrastructure in Seattle is being offered as a guide for developers and tech enthusiasts, providing an opportunity to spot various layers of data collection about one's life. The tour covers categories such as address, appearance, what the technology does, how it works, social importance, and discussion points. Surveillance cameras are highlighted as ubiquitous yet often overlooked, with examples including hotspots on poles and rooftops, as well as indoor cameras at banks and government buildings.
summary::Adafruit receives demand letter: Adafruit received a demand letter from Fenwick legal counsel on behalf of Flux.ai, alleging false and potentially defamatory claims about the company's intellectual property, commercial traction, and user base. The letter also asserts claims under the Computer Fraud and Abuse Act. Adafruit has temporarily stopped publishing on its blog while considering its response and next steps, citing a matter of public security interest.
summary::Until Dawn 2 looks like Cabin i: Until Dawn 2 is set to be released for PlayStation 5 in 2027 and appears to have all the trappings of its predecessor Cabin in the Woods but with a tropical island setting instead. The sequel features quick-time events and lethal consequences, similar to Until Dawn, and focuses on the teenage slasher aesthetic. Firesprite's approach to the mechanics emphasizes relationships within the crew, which could lead to unforeseen consequences.
summary::Onimusha: Way of the Sword arri: Onimusha: Way of the Sword is set to release on September 25 but a demo is available today allowing players to experience some of the game's features and gameplay mechanics before the full release. The demo includes a showpiece boss fight atop Mount Oe, which hints at the story context for how protagonist Miyamoto Musashi obtained his oni powers. This new trailer also gives fans a glimpse into the game's fighting mechanics and the supernatural abilities that Mushashi can channel in combat.
summary::4K years ago, Mohenjo-daro grew: Researchers from the University of York have found that Mohenjo-daro, an ancient city in present-day Pakistan, grew more equal over time by examining housing patterns and economic inequality levels. The study suggests that as the city developed, inequality dropped instead of rising, with house sizes becoming more similar during later periods of growth. This finding challenges the long-held assumption that cities grow alongside inequality, and highlights the importance of practical systems in limiting social disparities.
summary::Use your Nvidia GPU&#x27;s VRAM: Nvidia GPU's VRAM can be used as a swap space on Linux by allocating it via the CUDA driver API and serving it as a block device using the NBD protocol over a Unix socket. This allows for efficient use of the available VRAM, particularly in laptops with soldered memory and no upgrade path.
summary::Gleam v1.17.0: Gleam v1.17.0 has been released, bringing improvements to its BEAM (Erlang-based) programming model and escript (JavaScript runtime bundle) functionality. The new release includes a gleam export escript command that compiles projects, verifies main functions, and builds escripts from compiled bytecode. This feature simplifies the process of creating JavaScript bundles for small command-line programs.
summary::Microsoft CEO: We’re moving fro: Microsoft is shifting its focus from operating systems and apps to agents in an effort to create a more personal and aware computing experience. The company's CEO Satya Nadella has stated that this shift will be driven by the need for "agents" to interact with users, rather than traditional software platforms. This move is part of Project Solara, a new chip-to-cloud platform that aims to power AI experiences in a more seamless and integrated way.
summary::Now AI agents need what RSS doe: RSS provides a predictable and structured way for AI agents to consume new content, including podcast episodes, written articles, and newsletters, by offering a deterministic list of what is new without rate limits or authentication walls. This is in contrast to social platform APIs that often revoke access on a quarterly basis and charge for it. As a result, RSS is well-suited for applications such as language model retrieval, monitoring agent checks, and summarisation tools, making it an essential protocol for developers working with AI agents.
summary::Codex Discovered a Hidden HTTP/: Codex Discovered a Hidden HTTP/2 Bomb Jun 02, 2026 at 3:01 AM We're publishing an attack I missed that targets HPACK, HTTP/2's header compression scheme, which can lead to denial-of-service exploits. The bomb is chained from two techniques known to humans for over a decade and can consume server memory on home computers within seconds.
summary::Full Disclosure: 1-Click GitHub: Your browser is quite old and has a vulnerability that allows an attacker to steal a GitHub token by clicking on a link or using a malicious VSCode extension. This vulnerability exploits the fact that VSCode can run arbitrary JavaScript in its webview, which is isolated from the main application window but still vulnerable to cross-origin policy restrictions.
summary::My Students Can&#x27;t Read: Researchers have developed an AI-powered tool that can detect and correct errors in code written by students, helping to improve coding skills. The technology uses machine learning algorithms to analyze code and identify mistakes, providing feedback on syntax, semantics, and best practices. By automating the process of error detection, developers can focus on higher-level tasks, such as debugging and optimization.
summary::AI Outperforms Law Professors i: Law professors overwhelmingly preferred AI-generated answers over peer-written ones in a Stanford Law School study, finding that AI systems performed comparably to human instructors in evaluating contract law questions. The study's results challenge assumptions about AI's role in legal education, highlighting its ability to reason and provide nuanced explanations of complex material.
summary::More than 6 out of 10 people tu: More than 6 out of 10 people turn to artificial intelligence for psychological support due to screens' negative impact on mental health, yet technology can also play a positive role by providing helpful advice and resources. A global study found that 42% of respondents use AI for mental health questions, with almost all following the advice given. This highlights the need for companies to support individuals in their mental well-being, particularly in times of stress.
summary::LLMs are not the black box you: LLMs are not the "black box" you were promised, as they have made significant strides in mechanistic interpretability efforts. Anthropic's On the Biology of a Large Language Model (2025) provides a landmark in this effort, revealing how LLMs can be understood to think and reason about high-level concepts. By decomposing base model activations into sparse features that correspond to human-interpretable concepts, models can engage in genuine multi-step reasoning via intermediary concepts. This phenomenon is not unique to language models, with MCTS-based systems like AlphaZero also converging on concepts that humans recognize.
summary::Capstone – multi-platform, mult: Capstone is a lightweight multi-platform, multi-architecture disassembly framework that provides a clean, simple, and lightweight API for disassembling instructions from binary files. Its key feature is its ability to handle various architectures, including ARM, ARM64, BPF, Ethereum VM, M68K, M680X, Mips, MOS65XX, PowerPC, RISC-V, SH, Sparc, SystemZ, TMS320C64X, TriCore, Webassembly, XCore, and X86. Capstone is implemented in pure C language with bindings for several programming languages, making it a versatile tool for reverse engineering and malware analysis. Its high performance, thread-safety, and support for embedding into firmware or OS kernels make it an attractive option for developers.
summary::AI outperforms law professors i: Law professors overwhelmingly preferred AI-generated answers over peer-written ones in a Stanford Law School study, finding that AI systems performed comparably to human instructors in evaluating complex contracts law questions. The study, which involved 16 law professors across US law schools, tested whether large language models could serve as effective tutors for contract law courses and found that AI responses were rated significantly higher than those written by other professors. This finding challenges assumptions about AI's role in legal education and highlights the need to consider its limitations when evaluating complex arguments.
summary::Roku LT Operating System open s: The Roku LT operating system, a custom version of Linux, is now available as an open-source distribution for developers. This allows them to modify and customize the software to suit their needs. The open-source nature enables developers to contribute to the project and potentially create new features or applications. By making it possible to distribute modified versions of the OS, this technology can help advance the development of smart TV platforms.
summary::Pluto.jl 1.0 release – reactive: Pluto 1.0 release! Package Announcements pluto , notebooks fonsp May 27, 2026, 3:18pm After six years, it's time to release Pluto version 1.0, marking the culmination of progress over the past years and symbolising that Pluto is ready. This release brings significant improvements in reproducibility and reliability, sharing work, reactivity, interactivity, accessibility, education, AI tools, documentation, and an ecosystem.
summary::1-Click GitHub Token Stealing v: Your browser is quite old and can be upgraded to a different one for better experience on this site. By clicking a link, it's possible for an attacker to steal a GitHub token that can read and write to your private repositories using the VSCode webview security model. The bug allows attackers to access full access to every other repository you have access to, even if they interacted with a specific repo previously. This vulnerability is due to the presence of an OAuth token in the GitHub dev instance running inside the VSCode webview.
summary::Are blue zones real? Answering: Skepticism over the concept of blue zones is growing as researchers struggle to replicate findings from earlier studies and numbers of healthy seniors in original regions have plummeted. The term, coined by Belgian demographer Michel Poulain and Italian physician Giovanni Pes in the early 2000s, refers to areas with exceptionally high life expectancy where residents eat simple foods and engage in active habits. Despite Buettner's efforts to trademark the term, it has become a widely used label for wellness spaces and products claiming to promote longevity.
summary::Agentic Mfw: This website is built using Shit, a lightweight and fast codebase that loads quickly on all devices with one clamp(). The site also has semantic HTML, legible typography, and accessibility features to make it usable by everyone, regardless of their screen reader or browser. However, the developers behind this project have moved away from clean code principles in favor of complexity and scalability, prioritizing maintainability over simplicity and focusing on generating revenue through token sales rather than building maintainable websites.
summary::Cave of Forgotten Dreams: AI's influence on professional communication has become increasingly transparent, with leaders sharing their thoughts and opinions directly into AI-driven chatbots, often without engaging with human feedback. This raises questions about the nature of authenticity in digital interactions, as individuals may feel compelled to adapt their responses to avoid being perceived as "wrong" or "liars". The author's experience highlights the complexities of navigating these new norms, where even seemingly innocuous interactions can have profound implications for one's professional reputation.
summary::The Unreasonable Redundancy of: Deep learning models have made significant strides in predicting biomolecular interactions, including drug-protein and antibody-protein complexes. However, the current approach of scaling up model complexity and computing power has yielded limited results in terms of generating novel molecular structures. Researchers are now exploring ways to improve their systems by scaling down model complexity and increasing data quality, such as using structure prediction models to convert sequence scale into structural data. This approach may lead to more diverse and effective designs for drug-like molecules.
summary::Gleam v1.17 - Single file Gleam: Gleam v1.17.0 has been released, bringing improvements for single file Gleam BEAM programs with escript. The new release includes a gleam export escript command that compiles and builds an escript from compiled bytecode, making it easier to share small command-line programs. This feature is particularly useful in the JavaScript world where bundling multiple modules into a single file can be cumbersome.
summary::rsync and outrage: rsync, a popular command-line tool for transferring files between hosts, has been hijacked by a group of hackers who are using it to distribute malware. The attack involves exploiting a vulnerability in the rsync library that allows attackers to upload malicious code to compromised systems. As a result, developers should exercise caution when using rsync and ensure their systems have up-to-date security patches. This highlights the importance of keeping software libraries and command-line tools secure.
summary::Microsoft Doubles Down on Contr: Microsoft has doubled down on its claims about quantum computing, stating that its technology is being used to solve real-world problems. The company says its quantum computers are capable of simulating complex systems and solving certain types of optimization problems at speeds that are currently unachievable by classical computers. Microsoft's research into quantum computing has been met with skepticism from some in the industry, who question whether the technology can be scaled up for practical use.
summary::How TypeScript distributes unio: TypeScript's union type system allows it to distribute unions over multiple signatures of a single function, returning different types based on the input type. This can be achieved by using generic signatures or extractor types, which allow for more flexibility in defining functions with multiple return types. While TypeScript does not currently support true overload resolution like Java, its current implementation provides a way to write multiple type signatures for a single existing JavaScript function, and it generally shies away from complex code generation.
summary::Life and work is not meant to b: Life and work is not meant to be spent in isolation, a sentiment that has been echoed by many legends in the computer science space. Programmers such as Andrew Tridgell, Linus Torvalds, John Carmack, Chris Lattner, Miguel de Icaza, Fabrice Bellard, Mike Pall, Peter Schüller, Emil Tullstedt, Carl Öst Wilkens, Per Lundberg, and Lova Widmark are actively involved in the field and can be reached through various channels. This could inspire developers to reach out and connect with these individuals, potentially leading to new opportunities and experiences.
summary::Show HN: Edsger – A handwritten: Edsger - a handwritten Clojure REPL for the reMarkable 2 is an interesting tool that allows developers to interact with Clojure code directly on the e-ink display of the reMarkable 2. The technology behind Edsger is likely based on a custom implementation of the ReplClojure library, which provides a Clojure runtime environment for writing and executing code in a text-based interface. This feature can be useful for developers who prefer to work with Clojure in a more interactive and visual way.
summary::Writing Portable ARM64 Assembly: Writing portable ARM64 assembly code requires being aware of differences between Mach-O and ELF ABIs, as well as specific Apple-specific syntax extensions to avoid. This includes prefixing function names with a single underscore on Darwin, using different classes of data in ELF versus Mach-O, and understanding platform-specific register settings and vector mnemonics. By following the guidance outlined in this blog, developers can write assembly code that is portable between these tools and operating systems.
summary::Every Byte Matters: Every byte matters is about how the underlying hardware, specifically the CPU and its caches, affects performance in algorithms like iteration over complex data structures. This can have a significant impact on the time it takes to execute code, especially when dealing with large datasets or complex operations. Understanding the trade-offs between cache line sizes, number of cores, and instance sharing can help developers optimize their code for better performance.
summary::PlayStation Architecture: The PlayStation's architecture is based on a System-on-Chip, with the CPU being designed by Sony as part of a larger chip called the CXD8530BQ. This chip features a combination of RISC and CISC architectures, which allows for efficient processing of complex tasks. The design of this chip was influenced by previous Motorola 68k processors and the need to optimize performance in a relatively small package.
summary::Hacking your PC using your spea: Hacking your PC using your speaker without ever touching it is possible due to vulnerabilities in Creative Sound Blaster Katana V2X firmware. The device uses a proprietary protocol called CTP for communication over USB and requires challenge-response authentication before allowing commands to be executed, which can only be performed by static keys derived from the binaries that ship with the Creative App. Firmware analysis reveals that there are three parts to the firmware container: FBOOT, FMAIN, and CHK2, each containing significant value including a recovery mode for the speaker and a main firmware that is about 6.5 times larger than FBOOT.
summary::Mathematicians issue warning as: Researchers are sounding the alarm about the rapid advancement of artificial intelligence, which is outpacing human capabilities in many areas. The field's exponential growth has led to concerns that AI could surpass human intelligence and potentially disrupt critical systems. As a result, mathematicians have issued a warning about the need for caution and careful consideration when developing and deploying AI technologies.
summary::Take Action: LAPD Removed Crime: The Los Angeles Police Department has removed block-level crime location information from its open crime data feed, making it difficult for the public to determine where crimes occurred. This move restricts access to timely and accurate crime reporting, which is essential for residents, journalists, researchers, and community members to make informed decisions about safety. The removal of this critical information raises concerns about transparency and accountability, as independent watchdog organizations have also expressed dissatisfaction with the LAPD's data handling practices.
summary::Which sparkling water is the be: The best sparkling water for drinking varies depending on personal taste, but high-minerality waters like Numen and Badoit Intense tend to be more popular with tasters due to their higher fizz levels. A Bradley-Terry model analysis found that San Pellegrino came out on top in a head-to-head tournament against other top-tier options, while Perrier Fine Bulles was deemed the best value for money.
summary::32GB of DDR5 now costs $375 – A: 32GB of DDR5 memory now costs $375, a significant increase from its previous price. summary::Meta workers can opt out of bei: Meta is scaling back a plan to track employees' computer activity for up to 30 minutes at a time, allowing them to opt out or request exemptions from the initiative. The company received criticism from its own staff after announcing the tool in April, which was meant to train AI models by logging keystrokes and mouse clicks. Employees can now pause data collection for up to 30 minutes or request exemptions from the plan.
summary::Piramidal (YC W24) – Software E: Piramidal, a software engineering company based in New York City, is seeking senior engineers for their team. The company's backend and infrastructure stack is built using Python, Go, and Terraform, while its frontend stack relies on React and TypeScript. To be considered, applicants must have experience with reliable streaming systems or building smooth data-intensive UIs, as well as an active role in architecture and design implementation.
summary::.furry - A Top-Level Domain for: The application process for a new top-level domain (TLD), or .furry, is underway as ICANN begins to create a list of generic TLDs. The organization has accepted Pawprint Prototyping's hackerspace and non-profit, which will use funds raised through crowdfunding to cover the remaining costs required to bring the TLD to life. The .furry domain will be used for furries, making it easier for them to host their websites and online communities.
summary::WSL containers, Coreutils for W: Windows 11 quality is being raised through a foundation of strong fundamentals coupled with a developer experience that removes friction and provides flexibility across local and cloud, platforms, languages and frameworks. The platform is also becoming more secure and reliable by focusing on reducing cognitive load in various parts of the shell. Additionally, Windows is investing heavily in enabling developers to run AI workloads securely where they make sense, without compromising performance or functionality.
summary::Revealing the frontier with sta: Revealing the frontier with stacks and queues is a neglected super-power for developers. It's often a better way to see problems than recursion when trees and other graphs are involved. Tree traversal can be complex, especially on modern computers, but it shines in certain situations. Depth-first traversal in natural order is a common approach that lists files as they would appear with a standard tree representation. This method is easy to understand, but it comes with some hidden costs like stack allocation and early exit behavior. A stack-based function can be less efficient than its recursive counterpart, but it's still a useful technique to know about.
summary::A Post-Quantum Future for Let&#: Let's Encrypt is committed to a post-quantum-safe Web PKI using Merkle Tree Certificates as the new approach that adds post-quantum authentication without sacrificing speed and reliability. This move is worth pursuing due to its potential to create a post-quantum future, with an increasingly urgent problem of post-quantum cryptography being discussed in the context of encryption. The company's plans are driven by the need for post-quantum algorithms on national security systems, with the US NSA directing them towards post-quantum algorithms since 2022 and NIST's draft transition guidance deprecating RSA-2048 after 2030.
summary::Shopify Is Down: Shopify, one of the world's largest e-commerce platforms, has experienced significant downtime in recent days. Additionally, the latest version features improved performance and stability, making it easier to work on complex projects. summary::burnrsushi discusses personal m: Encephalitis is an autoimmune disorder where the body's normally helpful antibodies start acting strangely, leading to inflammation in the brain. This can result in symptoms such as anxiety, panic attacks, and physical problems like chronic jaw pain and balance issues. The condition often goes undiagnosed or misdiagnosed until it has progressed significantly, as was the case with the author who had suicidal ideation and psychosis before being diagnosed with anti-NMDA receptor encephalitis.
summary::mimalloc: A new, high-performan: mimalloc is a high-performance and scalable memory allocator that provides bounded worst-case allocation times and minimal contention, making it suitable for concurrent applications with hundreds of threads and large memory scales. It is an open-source drop-in replacement for malloc and free, with clear internal data structures and ease of integration into other projects.

summary::Fluid Simulation for Dummies: Fluid simulation for 3D graphics involves breaking down a complex fluid into a manageable number of boxes that interact with each other in real-time. This process is based on the Navier-Stokes equations, which describe how fluids behave under various forces and conditions. By simplifying these equations and using parallel processing techniques to simulate multiple interactions per second, developers can create realistic and immersive 3D fluid simulations for games and other applications.
summary::Elixir v1.20 released: now a gr: Elixir v1.20 released: now a gradually typed language June 03, 2026 by José Valim in Announcements Elixir v1.20 has been released, marking a milestone for the gradual type system introduced in 2022. This new version performs type inference and checks every program without introducing type annotations, ensuring dead code and verified bugs are found efficiently and with low false positives. The dynamic() type is a key component of this system, allowing it to narrow types at runtime and report typing violations that would fail at compile time.

summary::Angular v22: Angular 22 is a major update to Google's popular open-source framework for building web applications.
